titlestmt.titleproper: Keith Busch Northeast Ohio punk collection8152
titlestmt.author: RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-02-23 23:59:09 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Keith Busch Northeast Ohio punk collection

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Keith Busch Northeast Ohio Punk Archive documents the graphic and musical activities of zine-maker, designer, and musician Keith Busch, dating from 1979 to 1990. It contains approximately 300 unique sheets of original collage and mockup for zines, flyers, and posters; 19 complete zines; 82 Xerox flyers and mockup sheets; three original collage artworks; and 49 cassette tapes of music by Ragged Bags, live recordings of local shows, mix tapes, and other home recordings. The materials provide rich context for understanding the oppositional cultural community of the 1980s Ohio punk scene, with a specific focus on its graphic production and printed matter. It includes Busch's zines Le Canard Sauvage (Trout Gazette), Ragged Bags '83 Bag It Tour, Our Navy is Small, and Death, among others, as well as original collage artworks and maquettes showing various stages of editing, Xeroxing, and layout. Flyers designed by Busch document performances by Ragged Bags, The Tormentors, The Offbeats, Scared Guns, the Tokays, Death of Samantha, and others at venues such as JB's in Kent and The Bank in Akron. Busch also created annual collaged calendars and promotional materials for Kent record store Spindizzy. The collection examines the intersection of independent publishing, music performance, and visual design in regional punk subcultures. It situates Busch's work within the broader lineage of Northeastern Ohio's proto-punk and art movements. Materials outside of Busch's creative output, such as correspondence, personal papers, or documentation of other Ohio punk figures, are not present in the collection.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Keith Busch was a prolific creator of zines, show flyers, posters, and music. Publishing under the name Dag Nabit, he edited the seminal punk zine Boredom and produced several dozen other zines. Busch was a member of the Kent (Ohio) punk bands Ragged Bags, (co-founder in the early 1980s with Tommy Strange) and BAK2²1. He performed under both his own name and the moniker Back to Square One. In addition to his publishing and musical activities, Busch designed posters and flyers for bands and venues associated with the Akron and Kent alternative music scenes.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Keith Busch Northeast Ohio punk collection, #8152. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I. Collage and Zine Maquettes, Zines, Show Flyers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 Xeroxed sheets bound with ribbon to the top edge. —8 ½ x 14 inches.

did.unittitle: Emptying the Madhouse and Zine Maquettes and Originals of Death

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 collaged, double-sided sheets for Death zine, 8 ½ x 14 inches, and four double-sided collaged sheets for the zine Moving to New York. 8 ½ x 11 inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 collaged and hand-drawn sheets for the 1984 monthly calendar—8 ½ x 14 inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 collaged and hand-drawn sheets for the 1986 monthly calendar - 8 1/2 x 14 inches.

did.unittitle: Original collages and mock-ups for Flyers (Part 1)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 26 original collages for show flyers created by Keith Busch. Largely for shows at JB' s in Kent or the Bank in Akron, Ohio. 8 ½ x 11 inches - 8 ½ x 14 inches

did.unittitle: Original Collages and Mock-ups for Flyers (Part 2)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collaged sheet, for show flyers created by Keith Busch—8 ½ x 14 inches.

did.unittitle: Original mock-ups, collages, and zine maquettes (Part 1-3)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 200 sheets of original collages, mock-ups, and maquettes for various zines and projects, including several issues of Le Canard Sauvage, Our Navy is Small Parts 1 and 2, Negroes, The Boredom, The Ragged Bags: '83, Bag It Tour, The Lad Who Went to Mars, Tongue Dung, and several dozen sheets of maquettes and collages in various stages of completion. Collage with newspaper and magazine clippings, glue, pen, white-out, marker, and tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six collaged mock-ups for Spindizzy Records flyers and advertisements, and five Xeroxes for Spindizzy flyers and advertisements.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 28 sheets of Xeroxed production materials of various sizes, ranging from 8 ½ x 11 inches to 5 ½ x 7 inches, and Xeroxed layout materials for zines.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 36 Xeroxed show flyers created by Keith Busch. Largely for shows at JB' s in Kent or the Bank in Akron, Ohio. 8 ½ x 11 in. ca.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete Xeroxed zines created by Keith Busch. Contains the "'zines Death, three varying copies of The Lad Who Went to Mars, Emptying the Madhouse, two different copies of Tongue Dung, Kero/Wacky, Negroes, an untitled 1986 New York zine, Ragged Bags '83 Bag it Tour: A Read-Aloud Story, Is my nose Aflame? Or Our Navy is Small #1, and six issues of Le Canard Sauvage.

did.unittitle: Series II. Keith Busch Original Artworks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mixed media, paper clippings with glue, on scrap cardboard. Labeled and signed by Busch on verso in pencil. 11 ¼ x 16 ¼ inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mixed media, paper clippings, paint, plastic sheets, glitter, glue, Aspirin tablets, etc. On canvas board. 12 x 16 inches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mixed media, magazine and comics clippings, foil, glue, etc. On cardboard. Double-sided circular cut-out collage with a string affixed to the top for hanging. 12 x 12 inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compilation TDK Audio Cassette with handwritten label "Meathouse" with photocopied insert.

did.unittitle: Red Maxell UR60 Audio Cassette Box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 audio cassettes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dubbed from a TDK AD90 good sound EQ'd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bank 1980, June 27. JB's 1980, June 30.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith and Lisa Kamikaze read for Venus and BAk 221 practice. Trout Palace 1980.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BAK 221 practice 8 or 9, 1980. Dubbed from a salvaged Maxell LN C90. B: side of a Maxell LN C60.

did.unittitle: Keith Busch- the Jimmy Bell breakdown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: Live at the Warehouse. Their minds on cocaine. 1. Jimmy Bell's in Town Matchbox, Jimmy Bell's in town #2, The name game, Louie Louie, Gonorrhea blues, sea Cruise, Bad Yard cover. Keit and a friend out of dancing with Yer Disco kit. Louie Louie. Betty bop's ok.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3D house of Junk kent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side B: Backwards Goofin or fun for schizo's.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caution! Ancient Mallory Duratape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith, Revolution John, Mike Boals. Sp. Guest John Phlegm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fools on fire JB's 1st performance. Keith Busch - g + u , John Ongly - bass, Mike Boals drums + J. Phlegm m. several songs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 audio cassettes, with handwritten label and track listing on side "Keith Busch - Mighty Guitar Tapes"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: one of those tapes mid 70s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Latest hits of two bipeds who are strangling guitars circa 1979/1980.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two Bi peds strangling guitars

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Both sides dubbed from memory C-60s, Decent quality. Probably '75-76.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A and part of B. Dubbed from Maxell LN C60 marked "sounds from 1978 3 channel output with 1 Gadget". Rest of B: side are from a realistic chrome tape marked "Me jamming -not bad-sounds like 2 or 3 guitars. EQ'd very good sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a Memorex MRX2 oxide tape in a new housing. The original shell was heat / beer / drink damaged. Some gargly vocals on side 1. Sound - Fair to good.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mid 1970s. Probably 1976 or 1977. EQ'd: good sound. Cont. Double tracked stuff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dubbed from a Mallory Duratape. Good sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BAK 221 Jamming.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mooter cylinders and muffler scrapings

did.unittitle: Maxell UR60 teal Audio Cassette Box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 audio cassettes, with handwritten label and track listing on side "Keith Busch - BAK221, Concept+ Compilation Tapes 1995."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BAK 221 practice April 19, 1980 and etc records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Master of EQ'd stuff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch comp #2. BAK 221 rat, ksu '79. Mundenes JB's '80. Tormentors bank '81. BAK 221 bank '81. Juice ksu '82. Tokays '82. R.b's '83. RB's '83 + 84. Crumy fags '85. Loud persons 84 + 85. Dubbed from the original Bast c-90 EQ'd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch comp #1. Albatross live at Middle field hall '73 multi track guitar tunes from '74, 1st drum mach in his bedroom '79. Waffa Piyuck live in warehouse '78, studio cut '78 (all by k.) Cut done at home c. '78. BAK 221 above philis Tailor shop '79. Warehouse foolery '78 and '79. Dubbed from the BASF C90 original.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Making of "Good weird/Bad weird" #4 and street talk Columbus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Christmas in July '86. Recorded on the 15th of July.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wild drive tape June 21, 1986.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch with Albatross + Solo circa pwo two by Tin Huey!. Dubbed from a mallory Duro tape c-60. EQ'd pretty good sound considering its condition and age.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concept tape #2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch Zen Radio tape. Good trip tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 audio cassettes, with handwritten label and track listing on side "Keith Busch - Waffa Piyuck + Mighty Guitar 1995."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch. Me + Me (sounds fuzzy) circa 1979/1980.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch. "Drum crap with Jerk Bass" early 1980s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch Guitar Tracks waffa Piyuck. Realistic chrome tape side 2 Waffa dubbed from a c 4/5 TDK. EQ'd good sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Waffa Piyuck 2. Waffa Junior. EQ'd very good sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch. "Good session w/vocals" and misc records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch. Circa 1979-1980. Side A: Significant. Side B: Old me before drams at mother's house.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch. "This one" + "Here it is". Early 80s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Citric ASS-ID. August 9, 1979. 2nd half of a continuous 90 min Jam and it sounds like iT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dubbed from Memorex c-60s. Very good sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dubbed from a TDK SA 90, good sound, EQ'd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 audio cassettes, with handwritten label and track listing on side "Off Hours Projex + Party Bands 1995."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: BAK 221 era Guitar stuff. Original tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also has seen hill reading reading lend of side one/ stuff with Lisa kamikaze? About 10 versions of satisfaction + Lisa reads poultry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Massive foolery + fun dubbed from an extremely damaged sunset cassette probably cuz it's similar to Horizon Norelco tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Horns from hell- I'm Aman, drinking song. Loud poems "waiting for my man overture". "Let's get eaten by a shark". (1978), "Ferris wheel" (1978). With Tim Slabaugh. Ballad of the American farmer 1978. Collage (30) Jazz, radio/Tv ex's/ DOA," Dance on screen" etc.) Dubbed from a K-Mart C-60 sent to Randy Russell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch with Turtle Grumblings tape guitar tracks. EQ'd very good sound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dubbed from a Maxell UDS II. Made from the original tapes. These were done on a quad machine., so you are only getting two tracks. B side is same sequence (I think) but backwards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keith Busch vol II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "That Youngstown Band I played with" and "clarence" c.1977
